{{Youmay|the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]-era ship|its [[Chu'unthor|predecessor]]}}
The '''''Chu'unthor''''', the second ship to bear that name, was the wandering [[Jedi Praxeum|praxeum]] [[Starship/Legends|starship]] of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Djinn Altis/Legends|Djinn Altis]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].

==History==
It was named after its more famous [[Chu'unthor|predecessor]], which crashed on [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]] centuries before. Amongst its students were [[Callista Ming]] and [[Geith Eris|Geith]].<ref name="The New Essential Guide to Characters">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Aqinos]] taught on the ''Chu'unthor'' as well.<ref name="Droids, Technology and the Force: A Clash of Phenomena article">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/81/indexp7.html|text=Droids, Technology and the Force: A Clash of Phenomena|int=Droids, Technology and the Force: A Clash of Phenomena (article)}}</ref>

At some point, [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] found the ''Chu'unthor'' and recovered records from it, amongst them an account of the life of [[Aurra Sing/Legends|Aurra Sing]].<ref name="Legacy of the Force: Tempest">''[[Legacy of the Force: Tempest]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The second ''Chu'unthor'' was originally mentioned in [[Darksaber (novel)|''Darksaber'']]. Despite being similar in some details to the one described in ''[[The Courtship of Princess Leia]]'', this ''Chu'unthor'' was said to be active long after ''Courtship'' established it had crashed. ''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' resolved this by establishing that it was a second ''Chu'unthor'' named in honor of the first.
